text: I’ll use the graphify skill first because this is a codebase architecture question and the repo advertises a local graph. Then I’ll verify the important pieces directly against source files.
The graph is present, so I’m querying it for the admin-permissions slice first. After that I’ll pin the claims to exact controller, attribute, service, and DI/permission-definition source lines.

command: /bin/bash -lc 'graphify query "Explain how admin permission checks work for controller actions. Identify the main attributes, services, permission records, and registration points." --budget 3000'
/bin/bash -lc 'graphify query "Explain how admin permission checks work for controller actions. Identify the main attributes, services, permission records, and registration points." --budget 3000'

text: The first graph query surfaced the admin controller usage pattern but it’s too broad around “controller” and “records.” I’m narrowing with source search now, starting from `CheckPermission`, `IPermissionService`, and permission provider definitions.

I found the key entry points: controllers use `[AuthorizeAdmin]` at class/action level and `[CheckPermission(...)]` on actions. I’m reading those framework attributes and the permission service implementation now, plus a representative controller and custom plugin permission provider.

command: /bin/bash -lc "rg -n \"class .*CheckPermission|CheckPermissionAttribute|AuthorizeAdminAttribute|class .*AuthorizeAdmin|interface IPermissionConfigManager|class DefaultPermissionConfigManager|class PermissionConfig|class StandardPermission|class PermissionRecord\\b|class PermissionRecordCustomerRoleMapping\\b\" src/Libraries src/Presentation src/Plugins -S"

/bin/bash -lc "rg -n \"class .*CheckPermission|CheckPermissionAttribute|AuthorizeAdminAttribute|class .*AuthorizeAdmin|interface IPermissionConfigManager|class DefaultPermissionConfigManager|class PermissionConfig|class StandardPermission|class PermissionRecord\\b|class PermissionRecordCustomerRoleMapping\\b\" src/Libraries src/Presentation src/Plugins -S"

text: The repository has a lot of permission usages, so I’m focusing the evidence on the framework path and two representative usages: built-in admin permissions and plugin-defined permissions. This should keep the explanation accurate without drowning in every controller.

I’ve confirmed the action-level filter behavior: multiple `CheckPermission` attributes are allowed and the filter accepts if any named permission authorizes. I’m now checking how permission names become database records and how those records are mapped to customer roles.

The permission definitions are not only constants: `PermissionConfig` carries display name, system name, category, and default roles, while `PermissionRecord`/mapping are the persisted form. I’m reading the service methods that install and authorize them.
I have the authorization flow now. One remaining piece is registration: where `IPermissionService` is wired into DI and where `InsertPermissionsAsync()` is invoked during startup/install/updates.

command: /bin/bash -lc "rg -n \"interface INopStartup|class NopStartup|ConfigureServices\\(|AddScoped<|AddTransient<|AddSingleton<|RegisterDependencies|AddNop|AddNopServices|services\\.Add\" src/Libraries/Nop.Core src/Presentation/Nop.Web.Framework src/Presentation/Nop.Web src/Libraries/Nop.Services -S | rg \"Permission|Engine|Startup|ConfigureServices|AddScoped|AddTransient|AddSingleton|Service|Dependency\""

/bin/bash -lc "rg -n \"interface INopStartup|class NopStartup|ConfigureServices\\(|AddScoped<|AddTransient<|AddSingleton<|RegisterDependencies|AddNop|AddNopServices|services\\.Add\" src/Libraries/Nop.Core src/Presentation/Nop.Web.Framework src/Presentation/Nop.Web src/Libraries/Nop.Services -S | rg \"Permission|Engine|Startup|ConfigureServices|AddScoped|AddTransient|AddSingleton|Service|Dependency\""

text: There are two registration layers: DI registers the service, and app startup calls permission insertion. Plugin permission records participate by implementing `IPermissionConfigManager`; no explicit DI registration is needed for those because the service discovers implementations via the type finder.
I’m collecting exact line-numbered snippets for the final answer now: the attributes, service authorization methods, and permission record/config types.

command: /bin/bash -lc "nl -ba src/Libraries/Nop.Services/Security/PermissionConfig.cs | sed -n '1,60p'; nl -ba src/Libraries/Nop.Services/Security/IPermissionConfigManager.cs | sed -n '1,30p'; nl -ba src/Libraries/Nop.Core/Domain/Security/PermissionRecord.cs | sed -n '1,40p'; nl -ba src/Libraries/Nop.Core/Domain/Security/PermissionRecordCustomerRoleMapping.cs | sed -n '1,35p'"

/bin/bash -lc "nl -ba src/Libraries/Nop.Services/Security/PermissionConfig.cs | sed -n '1,60p'; nl -ba src/Libraries/Nop.Services/Security/IPermissionConfigManager.cs | sed -n '1,30p'; nl -ba src/Libraries/Nop.Core/Domain/Security/PermissionRecord.cs | sed -n '1,40p'; nl -ba src/Libraries/Nop.Core/Domain/Security/PermissionRecordCustomerRoleMapping.cs | sed -n '1,35p'"

text: One nuance: built-in admin controllers inherit `BaseAdminController`, so `[AuthorizeAdmin]` is inherited from the base class. Plugin admin controllers often inherit `BasePluginController`, so they add the admin attributes themselves.

Admin controller authorization is a two-layer check:

1. **Admin-area access**
   Built-in admin controllers inherit [BaseAdminController](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Presentation/Nop.Web/Areas/Admin/Controllers/BaseAdminController.cs:12), which applies `[Area(ADMIN)]`, antiforgery, IP validation, `[AuthorizeAdmin]`, vendor validation, and tab-saving filters. `AuthorizeAdminAttribute` is a `TypeFilterAttribute` that resolves `IPermissionService` and checks `StandardPermission.Security.ACCESS_ADMIN_PANEL`; failure returns `ChallengeResult` [AuthorizeAdminAttribute.cs](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Presentation/Nop.Web.Framework/Mvc/Filters/AuthorizeAdminAttribute.cs:11), [line 89](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Presentation/Nop.Web.Framework/Mvc/Filters/AuthorizeAdminAttribute.cs:89).

2. **Action-specific permissions**
   Actions are guarded with `[CheckPermission(...)]`. Example: `SmsController : BaseAdminController` uses `StandardPermission.Configuration.MANAGE_SMS_SETTINGS` on `Providers` and `MarkAsPrimaryProvider` [SmsController.cs](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Presentation/Nop.Web/Areas/Admin/Controllers/SmsController.cs:45). Plugin admin controllers that do not inherit `BaseAdminController` typically add the admin attributes themselves; `PollAdminController` declares `[Area]`, `[AuthorizeAdmin]`, etc., then uses both standard and plugin permissions [PollAdminController.cs](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Plugins/Nop.Plugin.Misc.Polls/Admin/Controllers/PollAdminController.cs:22), [line 111](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Plugins/Nop.Plugin.Misc.Polls/Admin/Controllers/PollAdminController.cs:111).

`CheckPermissionAttribute` is method-only and allows multiple attributes [CheckPermissionAttribute.cs](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Presentation/Nop.Web.Framework/Mvc/Filters/CheckPermissionAttribute.cs:15). It accepts either one permission name or an array [lines 25-40](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Presentation/Nop.Web.Framework/Mvc/Filters/CheckPermissionAttribute.cs:25). At runtime, its nested filter loops through the configured permission system names and authorizes the request if **any one** succeeds [lines 116-121](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Presentation/Nop.Web.Framework/Mvc/Filters/CheckPermissionAttribute.cs:116). If none succeeds, it returns HTML redirect, JSON, or plain text depending on configured/default result type and request method [lines 130-168](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Presentation/Nop.Web.Framework/Mvc/Filters/CheckPermissionAttribute.cs:130).

The main service is `IPermissionService`/`PermissionService`. It is registered in DI as scoped in framework startup [NopStartup.cs](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Presentation/Nop.Web.Framework/Infrastructure/NopStartup.cs:168). `NopEngine` discovers all `INopStartup` implementations and calls `ConfigureServices` [NopEngine.cs](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Libraries/Nop.Core/Infrastructure/NopEngine.cs:110).

Authorization is role based. `AuthorizeAsync(string)` gets the current customer, loads their roles, and succeeds if any role has the permission [PermissionService.cs](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Libraries/Nop.Services/Security/PermissionService.cs:291), [lines 305-319](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Libraries/Nop.Services/Security/PermissionService.cs:305). Role-level authorization is cached and compares the requested system name to the `PermissionRecord.SystemName` values mapped to that role [lines 331-344](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Libraries/Nop.Services/Security/PermissionService.cs:331). The role-to-permission query joins `PermissionRecord` to `PermissionRecordCustomerRoleMapping` [lines 63-74](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Libraries/Nop.Services/Security/PermissionService.cs:63).

The persisted permission records are simple:
`PermissionRecord` has `Name`, `SystemName`, and `Category` [PermissionRecord.cs](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Libraries/Nop.Core/Domain/Security/PermissionRecord.cs:6). `PermissionRecordCustomerRoleMapping` stores `PermissionRecordId` and `CustomerRoleId` [PermissionRecordCustomerRoleMapping.cs](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Libraries/Nop.Core/Domain/Security/PermissionRecordCustomerRoleMapping.cs:6).

Permission definitions start as `PermissionConfig`: display name, system name, category, and default customer role system names [PermissionConfig.cs](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Libraries/Nop.Services/Security/PermissionConfig.cs:10). Providers implement `IPermissionConfigManager.AllConfigs` [IPermissionConfigManager.cs](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Libraries/Nop.Services/Security/IPermissionConfigManager.cs:6). Built-in permission names live in `StandardPermission`; for example `Configuration.ManageSmsSettings` and `Security.AccessAdminPanel` [StandardPermission.cs](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Libraries/Nop.Services/Security/StandardPermission.cs:123), [line 145](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Libraries/Nop.Services/Security/StandardPermission.cs:145). Built-in configs are in `DefaultPermissionConfigManager`, including admin-panel access and SMS/settings/plugin permissions with default roles [DefaultPermissionConfigManager.cs](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Libraries/Nop.Services/Security/DefaultPermissionConfigManager.cs:15), [line 141](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Libraries/Nop.Services/Security/DefaultPermissionConfigManager.cs:141).

Registration/install happens at application start: `AppStartedConsumer` calls `_permissionService.InsertPermissionsAsync()` after confirming the database is installed [AppStartedConsumer.cs](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Presentation/Nop.Web.Framework/Infrastructure/AppStartedConsumer.cs:61), [line 70](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Presentation/Nop.Web.Framework/Infrastructure/AppStartedConsumer.cs:70). `PermissionService.InsertPermissionsAsync()` discovers all `IPermissionConfigManager` classes via `ITypeFinder`, instantiates them, gathers `AllConfigs`, filters out existing system names, and installs new records [PermissionService.cs](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Libraries/Nop.Services/Security/PermissionService.cs:398). Installation inserts `PermissionRecord`, ensures default customer roles exist, inserts mappings, and saves localized permission names [lines 130-164](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Libraries/Nop.Services/Security/PermissionService.cs:130).

Plugins plug into the same path. The polls plugin defines constants `Polls.View` and `Polls.Manage` [PollsDefaults.cs](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Plugins/Nop.Plugin.Misc.Polls/PollsDefaults.cs:56), exposes them through `PollPermissionConfigManager : IPermissionConfigManager` [PollPermissionConfigManager.cs](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Plugins/Nop.Plugin.Misc.Polls/Services/PollPermissionConfigManager.cs:9), and uses them in controller actions [PollAdminController.cs](/home/simon/repos/lorq-worktrees/base-default-graphify__neutral__admin-permissions__r1/src/Plugins/Nop.Plugin.Misc.Polls/Admin/Controllers/PollAdminController.cs:111).
